truth.papers.A64.title: Alloy Design, Combinatorial Synthesis, and Microstructure-Property Relations for Low-Density Fe-Mn-Al-C Austenitic Steels
truth.papers.A64.paper_scope: Designs low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic steels by combinatorial high-throughput methods, explores microstructure-property relations, TWIP and κ-carbide strengthening mechanisms, and hydrogen embrittlement susceptibility.

prediction.papers.A64.title: Alloy Design, Combinatorial Synthesis, and Microstructure–Property Relations for Low-Density Fe-Mn-Al-C Austenitic Steels
prediction.papers.A64.paper_scope: Recent developments in low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic steels covering combinatorial alloy design via rapid alloy prototyping, microstructure-property relations for TWIP and kappa-carbide strengthened grades, constitutive modeling of strain hardening, and hydrogen embrittlement susceptibility.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe30mn1.2c_al0.alloys_notes: Base composition for 30Mn TWIP steel group, no Al addition.
truth.alloys.alloy_fe30mn1.2c_al8.alloys_notes: Highest Al content in RAP Fe-30Mn-1.2C series, strong aging response due to κ-carbides.
truth.alloys.alloy_fe22mn0.6c.alloys_notes: Used for constitutive model validation (compression tests at various temperatures).
truth.alloys.alloy_fe26mn11al1.2c.aliases[0]: low-density κ-carbide steel for hydrogen study
truth.alloys.alloy_fe26mn11al1.2c.alloys_notes: κ-carbide hardened austenitic low-density steel, used for hydrogen embrittlement study.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn1.2c_al0.alloys_notes: Reference ternary base alloy for the RAP screening series.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn1.2c_al8.alloys_notes: Highest Al variant in the Fe-30Mn-1.2C-xAl RAP series; density-reduced and susceptible to kappa-carbide precipitation during aging.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe20mn0.4c_al8.alloys_notes: Dual-phase austenite-ferrite low-density TWIP steel variant.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe20mn0.4c_al11.alloys_note: Higher-Al variant in the Fe-20Mn-0.4C-xAl series; significant ferrite fraction and kappa-carbide tendency.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe26mn11al1.2c.alloys_notes: Kappa-carbide hardened austenitic low-density steel used for hydrogen embrittlement study.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe22mn0.6c.alloys_notes: Model TWIP alloy used for constitutive model validation (data from Ref. 50).

truth.processes.proc_homogenised_wq.descriptio: Homogenization at 1100 °C for 2 h under Ar, followed by water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_homogenised_wq.processes_: Applied to hot-rolled sheets before aging treatments.
truth.processes.proc_aging_450c_1h.description: Aging at 450 °C for 1 h in air, oil quenching.
truth.processes.proc_aging_500c_1h.description: Aging at 500 °C for 1 h in air, oil quenching.
truth.processes.proc_aging_550c_1h.description: Aging at 550 °C for 1 h in air, oil quenching.
truth.processes.proc_aging_600c_1h.description: Aging at 600 °C for 1 h in air, oil quenching.
truth.processes.proc_aging_450c_24h.descriptio: Aging at 450 °C for 24 h in air, oil quenching.
truth.processes.proc_aging_500c_24h.descriptio: Aging at 500 °C for 24 h in air, oil quenching.
truth.processes.proc_aging_550c_24h.descriptio: Aging at 550 °C for 24 h in air, oil quenching.
truth.processes.proc_aging_600c_24h.descriptio: Aging at 600 °C for 24 h in air, oil quenching.
truth.processes.proc_aging_600c_24h.processes_: Promotes strong κ-carbide precipitation in high-Al alloys.
truth.processes.proc_aging_400c_24h.descriptio: Aging at 400 °C for 24 h in air, oil quenching.
truth.processes.proc_as_quenched.description: As-quenched condition after hot rolling and reheating, without aging.
truth.processes.proc_as_quenched.processes_not: Corresponds to homogenized and water-quenched state for Fe-20Mn series.

prediction.processes.proc_homogenised_wq.descriptio: VIM melting under 400 mbar Ar, casting into Cu molds, hot rolling at 1100°C to 2 mm sheet, reheating to 1100°C and water quenching, homogenization at 1100°C for 2 h under Ar followed by water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_homogenised_wq.processes_: Rapid alloy prototyping (RAP) synthesis route used for Fe-30Mn-1.2C-xAl and Fe-20Mn-0.4C-xAl bulk combinatorial screening.
prediction.processes.proc_aging_600c_24h.descriptio: Aging at 600°C for 24 h in air followed by oil quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_aging_600c_24h.processes_: Applied to selected Fe-30Mn-1.2C-xAl alloys to precipitate kappa-carbides.
prediction.processes.proc_aging_550c_24h.descriptio: Aging at 550°C for 24 h in air followed by oil quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_aging_550c_24h.processes_: Intermediate aging condition producing significant strengthening in high-Al variants.
prediction.processes.proc_as_quenched.description: Reheating to 1100°C and water quenching; homogenized as-quenched state without subsequent aging.
prediction.processes.proc_as_quenched.processes_not: Baseline condition for Fe-20Mn-0.4C-xAl series prior to tempering.
prediction.processes.proc_h_charging.description: Hydrogen precharging for 40 min plus permanent in situ hydrogen charging imposed during ongoing tensile tests.
prediction.processes.proc_h_charging.processes_note: Direct hydrogen charging approach used to study hydrogen embrittlement susceptibility.
prediction.processes.proc_compression_temp.descript: Compression testing at seven temperatures from 293 K to 873 K with a single coherent parameter set.
prediction.processes.proc_compression_temp.processe: Validation experiments for the temperature-sensitive constitutive model using a Fe-22Mn-0.6C TWIP model alloy.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_30mn0al_homog.pr: Homogenized under Ar atmosphere followed by water quenching; as-homogenized reference state.
Aging in air followed by oil quenching; scales removed by low-pressure fine-grit sandblasting.

truth.structures.sample_30mn8al_homog.overall_: Fully recrystallized austenite with some retained microsegregations of Mn.
truth.structures.sample_30mn8al_600c24h.overal: Austenitic matrix containing nanosized κ-carbide precipitates.

prediction.structures.sample_30mn0al_homog.overall_: Fully recrystallized single-phase austenite with equiaxed grains
prediction.structures.sample_30mn8al_homog.overall_: Fully recrystallized single-phase austenite; minor retained microsegregations of Mn
prediction.structures.sample_30mn8al_600c24h.overal: Austenitic matrix with regularly arranged nanosized kappa-carbide precipitates
prediction.structures.sample_fe26mn11al12c_h.overal: Kappa-carbide hardened austenite with hydrogen-induced damage
prediction.structures.sample_fe26mn11al12c_h.micros: planar slip localization and grain boundary voids

truth.interfaces.sample_30mn8al_600c24h.interf: Orowan bypassing of κ-carbide rods observed; dislocations accumulate in wide channels.
Shearing of κ-carbides occurs only at high stress concentrations.
truth.interfaces.sample_30mn8al_600c24h.phase_: κ-carbides precipitate during aging at 450-600°C; remain stable up to 100 h at 600°C.

prediction.interfaces.sample_30mn8al_600c24h.defect: Dislocation bypassing and shearing of kappa-carbide rods
Orowan bypassing of longitudinal rods with dislocation loop expansion assisted by cross-slip; minor shearing of kappa-carbide interfaces under high local stress
prediction.interfaces.sample_30mn8al_600c24h.phase_: Kappa-carbides thermally stable at 600°C, persisting after 100 h with only modest coarsening; form during aging at 500–600°C or during quenching for Al > 10 wt.%.
prediction.interfaces.sample_fe26mn11al12c_h.interf: Pronounced planar slip localization leads to high stress concentrations at grain boundaries
Hydrogen-assisted planar slip promotes grain boundary void coalescence and intergranular cracking

prediction.performance.sample_fe26mn11al12c_h.servi: Hydrogen charging (precharging 40 min + permanent in situ charging during tensile testing)

truth.characterization_methods.sample_30mn8al_: Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) + Atom Probe Tomography (APT)
κ-carbides have L'12 structure, composition (Fe,Mn)3AlC, size ~nm, arranged as rods.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe26mn11: Intergranular cracking, void chains on grain boundaries, intense planar slip localization linked to κ-carbides.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_30mn8al_: Chemical compositional analysis of nanosized precipitates
Kappa-carbide exhibits approximate stoichiometry (Fe,Mn)3AlC with C ~5 at.% and Fe/Mn/Al enrichments matching the L'1₂ structure.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe26mn11: Kappa-carbides act as hydrogen trap sites with estimated activation energy of ~76–80 kJ/mol from Redlich-Kister analysis.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe22mn06: Experimental true stress-strain and strain-hardening curves matched simulation across 293K–873K using a single coherent parameter set.

truth.computational_details.sample_fe22mn0.6c_: Compared simulated and experimental flow curves for Fe-22Mn-0.6C TWIP steel at 7 temperatures (293K–873K); one coherent parameter set used. Good agreement shown in Fig. 7.
Temperature-sensitive constitutive model for fcc metals with low SFE and mechanical twinning
b=2.56 Å, M=3.06, n=3, f_c=0.9, f_w=0.1, D0=4.0e-5 m²/s, average twin width e=30 nm
Based on Steinmetz et al. (2013) extension of Roters et al. model; includes twin nucleation probability, Orowan bypassing of κ-carbides suggested but not yet implemented.

prediction.computational_details.sample_fe22mn06c_v: Crystal plasticity constitutive model for fcc metals with low stacking fault energy and mechanical twinning; three-internal-variable dislocation density framework with twin nucleation

Validated against experimental true stress-strain compression curves and strain-hardening curves of Fe-22wt.%Mn-0.6wt.%C TWIP steel at temperatures from 293 K to 873 K using one coherent parameter set.
Steinmetz-Roters temperature-sensitive twinning dislocation density model
Evolution of dislocation densities rho_c, rho_w, rho_d and twin volume fraction f_tw; mean free path including twin and grain boundary contributions; glide resistance and activation energy for slip
b=2.56 Å, M=3.06, n=3, D0=4.0×10^-5 m²/s, fc=0.9, fw=0.1, twin width e=30 nm, p and q fitting parameters, Qs activation energy for slip, Qc vacancy formation and migration energy

Extends Roters three-internal-variable model with Mahajan-Chin twin nucleation; harmonic mean free path for cell interior and walls; resolved shear stress calculated separately for cell interior and walls; can be extended to include kappa-carbide Orowan looping.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Density reduction as function of Al content: Fe-Mn-Al-C TWIP steels with ~3% Al → density 7.25 g/cm³ (~8% reduction), ~6% Al → 7.10 (~10%), ~8% Al → 6.90 (~12%), ~11% Al → 6.60 (~15%), ~12% Al → 6.55 (~16%), ~13% Al → 6.50 (~17%). (Fig. 1)
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Fig. 2: Specific energy absorption E_spec (J/mm³) for Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys reaches ~0.54 J/mm³, outperforming high-strength IF steel (~0.25) and HC300LA (~0.33).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Fig. 3 complete RAP mechanical data (YS, UTS, TE, hardness) for Fe-30Mn-1.2C-xAl (x=0,2,4,6,8) after different aging treatments (as-homogenised, 450°C/1h, 500°C/1h, 550°C/1h, 600°C/1h, 550°C/24h, 600°C/24h, 450°C/24h, 500°C/24h). Key values: 0Al as-homog YS ~370 MPa, UTS ~830 MPa, TE ~70%; 8Al as-homog YS ~510 MPa, UTS ~750 MPa, TE ~72%; 8Al 600°C/24h YS ~950 MPa, UTS ~930 MPa, TE ~38%, hardness ~360 HV.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Fig. 4: Selected tensile data for Fe-30Mn-1.2C-xAl (aging condition not explicit); 8Al sample shows UTS ~950 MPa and elongation ~25%, while 0Al shows ~500 MPa and ~70% elongation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Fig. 6a mechanical data for Fe-20Mn-0.4C-xAl (x=0,2,5,8,11) after as-quenched and aging 400°C/24h, 500°C/24h, 600°C/24h. YS and UTS increase, TE decreases with Al. As-quenched values: 0Al YS 380 MPa, UTS 640 MPa, TE 30%; 11Al YS 700 MPa, UTS 800 MPa, TE 15%. XRD in Fig. 6b shows increasing ferrite content with Al, and emergence of κ-carbides.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Hydrogen embrittlement study on Fe-26Mn-11Al-1.2C: tensile ductility drastically reduced under hydrogen charging; two damage mechanisms identified: grain boundary triple junction cracking and void chains along grain boundaries due to planar slip localization. Thermal desorption analysis indicates κ-carbides act as hydrogen trap sites with activation energy ~76–80 kJ/mol (Redlich–Kister analysis). (Fig. 8, 9)
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Strain hardening model (equations 1–29) for low-SFE TWIP steels successfully reproduces experimental stress–strain curves for Fe-22Mn-0.6C between 293K and 873K.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: κ-carbide characteristics: ordered L'12 structure, approximate stoichiometry (Fe,Mn)3AlC, cuboidal/rod-like morphology, lattice mismatch <3% with austenite, semi-coherent with ferrite (~6%). Precipitates remain stable at 600°C for 100 h with modest coarsening.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: As-cast microstructure is coarse dendritic; after hot rolling and water quenching, fully recrystallized grain size ~20 µm with retained Mn microsegregations.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Combinatorial high-throughput RAP method synthesized 5 different alloys per base system (Fe-30Mn-1.2C-xAl and Fe-20Mn-0.4C-xAl) in one operation, followed by matrix of 9 aging conditions, enabling rapid screening within 35 h.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fig. 4 selected tensile strength for Fe-30Mn-1.2C-0Al (~500 MPa) deviates from Fig. 3b and text (~820–830 MPa as-homogenized), likely reflecting different data selections or conditions.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Fe-30Mn-1.2C-8Al exhibits pronounced age strengthening during 500–600°C aging; after 24 h at 600°C, YS ~830 MPa, UTS ~930 MPa, hardness ~330, and TE drops to ~10% due to nanosized kappa-carbide precipitation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Kappa-carbides in Fe-Mn-Al-C steels show high thermal stability at 600°C, persisting after 100 h with only modest coarsening, and possess a wide compositional existence range.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: For Al > 10 wt.%, kappa-carbides can form during quenching; the exact strain-hardening mechanism for as-quenched kappa-carbide steels remains under investigation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Low-density TWIP (SIMPLEX) steels with 25–30 wt.% Mn and <4–5 wt.% Al deform by dislocation hardening followed by massive mechanical twinning, providing specific energy absorption up to ~0.54 J/mm³, exceeding conventional deep-drawing steels.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Hydrogen embrittlement in Fe-26Mn-11Al-1.2C involves grain boundary triple junction cracking and microvoid chain formation along grain boundaries linked to planar slip localization induced by kappa-carbides.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Fig. 8 EBSD/ECCI data (inverse pole figure, KAM map, orientation-optimized ECCI) reveal slip localization and grain boundary damage in hydrogen-charged low-density steel, but pixel-level image details are not provided in the text pipeline.
